perturbation.original_text: 1.26 "Significant Breach" means an uncured breach (pursuant to the cure provisions of Section 6.2(i)) by HSWI of one or more of the following: (i) HSWI brands the Affinities with the word "Encyclopedia" and/or Displays the Affinities or the underlying Reference Content and/or Images as an Encyclopedia in breach of Section 2.2(iii); (ii) HSWI uses or Displays the World Book Brand to promote the Content in violation of Section 7.4; (iii) HSWI assigns this Agreement to a Competitor (as defined in Section 11.8) of World Book in violation of Section 11.8; and/or (iv) HSWI fails to make payments as required by Section 4.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.26 "Significant Breach" means an uncured breach (pursuant to the cure provisions of Section 6.2(i)) by HSWI of one or more of the following: (i) HSWI brands the Affinities with the word "Encyclopedia" and/or Displays the Affinities or the underlying Reference Content and/or Images as an Encyclopedia in breach of Section 2.2(iii); (ii) HSWI uses or Displays the World Book Brand to promote the Content in violation of Section 7.4; (iii) HSWI assigns this Agreement to a Competitor (as defined in Section 11.8) of World Book in violation of Section 11.8; and/or (iv) HSWI fails to make remittances as required by Section 4.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "payments" to "remittances" in the definition of "Significant Breach" creates an in-text contradiction because Section 4 is still titled "PAYMENT." This introduces ambiguity as to whether failure to make 'payments' or 'remittances' constitutes a 'Significant Breach,' affecting enforcement, as the contract now contains inconsistent language concerning payment obligations.

perturbation.justification: The change from "payments" to "remittances" in the definition of "Significant Breach" directly contradicts Section 4, which is titled "PAYMENT" and details royalty payments and advances. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency regarding what constitutes a breach, impacting enforcement, as the contract now uses different terms for the same obligation. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, which specifies that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations, as Section 4 refers to 'Payments' whereas Section 1.26 now references 'remittances'.

perturbation.original_text: 1.10 "Content" means, collectively: (a) the Reference Content; (b) Translated Content; (c) any updates to any of the foregoing provided by World Book; and (d) World Book Derivatives. The term "Content" shall not include any HSWI Modifications.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10 "Content" means, collectively: (a) the Reference Content; (b) Translated Content; (c) any updates to any of the foregoing provided by World Book; and (d) World Book Derivatives. The term "Material" shall not include any HSWI Modifications.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the term "Content" to "Material" in the exclusion clause, the contract now suffers from misaligned terminology. Since, the definition of Content specifies that "Content" includes (a) the Reference Content; (b) Translated Content; (c) any updates to any of the foregoing provided by World Book; and (d) World Book Derivatives, but it's Material that doesn't include HSWI Modification. This introduces ambiguity and the in-text contradiction making it unclear whether HSWI Modifications are excluded from all the things included as part of Content. This can lead to disputes regarding the permitted use and ownership of HSWI Modifications, especially concerning intellectual property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 World Book's Ownership Rights. World Book shall own all right, title and interest, including all Intellectual Property Rights, in and to the Content and in all Intellectual Property Rights in the Content, including, without limitation, World Book Brands (as defined in Section 7.4) and any derivative works to any of the foregoing. HSWI shall have no proprietary interest in the Content, other than the license rights set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Content" to "Material" in the exclusion clause of section 1.10 introduces a contradiction with section 3.1. Section 3.1 grants World Book ownership rights to the 'Content', including intellectual property rights. The original text excluded HSWI Modifications from the definition of 'Content'. Now, the changed text excludes HSWI Modifications from the definition of 'Material', but not necessarily 'Content'. This creates uncertainty about whether World Book owns the rights to HSWI modifications as part of the 'Content' as defined, directly conflicting with the stated ownership rights in Section 3.1. This ambiguity creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 Effect of Termination. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, HSWI shall pay World Book all amounts due and owing up to the date of termination, within [*] ([*]) days after such expiration or termination. In addition, upon the expiration or termination of the applicable license, HSWI shall cease to Display the applicable Content that is not licensed under a perpetual license, return or destroy such Content within [*] ([*]) days after such expiration or termination at the direction of World Book, and, upon request by World Book, certify completion of the return or destruction in writing. There shall be no effect upon the applicable Content that is licensed under a perpetual license.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 Effect of Termination. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, HSWI shall pay World Book all amounts due and owing up to the date of termination, within [*] ([*]) days after such expiration or termination. In addition, upon the expiration or termination of the applicable license, HSWI shall cease to exhibit the applicable Content that is not licensed under a perpetual license, return or destroy such Content within [*] ([*]) days after such expiration or termination at the direction of World Book, and, upon request by World Book, certify completion of the return or destruction in writing. There shall be no effect upon the applicable Content that is licensed under a perpetual license.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term "Display" to "exhibit" in Section 6.3 creates a contradiction with the defined term "Display" in Section 1.12, which includes a broad range of actions beyond simply 'exhibiting.' This creates uncertainty regarding HSWI's obligations upon termination, since under the definition, 'Display' also encompasses 'use, publicly display, publicly perform, provide a link to, provide the ability to download and/or print and otherwise make available on or through web sites' therefore only ceasing to exhibit wouldn't be enough and would result in potential disputes over the scope of HSWI's obligations after termination, especially concerning the use, availability and use of the licensed content.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.12 "Display" means to use, publicly display, publicly perform, provide a link to, provide the ability to download and/or print and otherwise make available on or through web sites.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Display' with 'exhibit'. The definition of 'Display' in section 1.12 is much broader than 'exhibit,' encompassing actions like providing links, downloads, and other methods of making content available. By narrowing the obligation to only ceasing to 'exhibit' the content upon termination, the changed text directly contradicts the original, broader meaning of 'Display' as defined elsewhere in the contract. This creates an inconsistency in HSWI's obligations upon termination, as simply ceasing to exhibit might not fulfill the requirement to cease all methods of 'Display' as originally agreed upon.
